objective: Design and implement a zero-loss, reproducible system with code persistence, state backup, and environment reconstruction to prevent total data loss in case of system failure.
decisions: Adopt a 3-layer architecture: Code Persistence Layer, State Persistence Layer, Environment Reproducibility Layer
Use Git and GitHub for code persistence
Use .env.example instead of committing .env for security
Use .gitignore to exclude sensitive and unnecessary files
Create backup folder and backup.sh for state persistence
Define daily workflow using git add, commit, push
Define weekly backup execution using backup.sh
Implement disaster recovery via git clone and setup.sh
Separate Redis as temporary storage, not primary persistence
Validate system through full recovery testing on new environment
Establish governance rules: never commit secrets, always push code, maintain dependency file
inputs: User concern about laptop crash and data loss
User request for full professional system setup
User inquiry about existence of system layers
User instruction to perform forensic extraction
outputs: Explanation of risk and system failure scenario
notes: All components, workflows, and scripts have been designed and documented but no execution has been confirmed by the user. System currently exists as a planned architecture without validated implementation.
